business.headline: The first 30 days of FY27: 7 smart money moves to make in AprilThe new financial year has just begun, and April is the time to take charge. The decisions you make now can shape your money journey for the rest of the year. In our new 'MONEY MAKEOVER' series, we break down simple, smart steps to get started right.

Tech layoffs: Is AI replacing jobs or are companies fixing overhiring?Job cuts have been reported across tech companies, showing that the trend is not limited to a single type of company. The trend has raised a key question. Are companies correcting overhiring from the past, or is artificial intelligence starting to reduce the need for certain roles?

DA hike: How much will your salary and pension increase this time?With the next dearness allowance (DA) hike expected soon, central government employees and pensioners are watching closely. Even a small rise can push up monthly income, but how much extra can you really expect this time?

Markets down, rupee weak. Why Iran war may now hit your home loanThe impact of Iran war has not been limited to Dalal Street. The rupee has also come under pressure as oil prices moved higher. Now, the ripple effect of the conflict could reach households, especially those with home loans.

Iran war hit your portfolio? Track these 4 signals to stay ahead on Dalal StreetFor investors, the bigger challenge is not just falling markets, but the confusion around what to track. Headlines are changing rapidly, with mixed signals on escalation, oil prices and supply disruptions. This makes it difficult to take clear investment decisions.

Getting 8.2% on small savings? Don't invest before these 5 key checksGetting returns of up to 8.2% on small savings schemes may sound like an easy win. But before you lock your money in, it's worth pausing to check a few basics that could impact your finances later.

Income tax filing 2026: Which 5 key links should every taxpayer check now?Filing your income tax return in 2026 may feel a bit different this time. With the portal revamped and new rules in place, knowing where to click can save both time and effort. Here are five key links you should not miss.

Layoffs everywhere, but this company says no job cutsThe company has said it will not cut jobs despite ongoing global uncertainty. It has committed to retaining its workforce and continuing salary payments without delay.
Good Friday 2026: Is stock market open or closed today?Both stock exchanges, the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) and the National Stock Exchange (NSE), are closed on April 3 on account of Good Friday.

Why buying diamonds is expensive but fails as an investmentThe biggest issue with diamonds is the resale value. Unlike gold, which can be sold easily at market-linked prices, diamonds do not have a strong resale market.

Govt puts mandatory 60% free airline seat rule on hold amid jet fuel price riseThe government has put on hold its directive requiring airlines to offer at least 60% of seats without additional charges, pending further review.

Economic costs are increasing every day: Dr Sajjid Chinoy on impact of Iran warDr Sajjid Z Chinoy, MD and Chief India Economist at JP Morgan, discussed the escalating economic impact of the war on global and Indian markets.

India imposes 25% cap on monthly jet fuel price increase for domestic flightsIndia imposes 25% cap on monthly jet fuel price increase for domestic flights, Reuters reports citing an official statement

Good Friday holiday: Will NSE and BSE be open or closed on April 3?The stock market holiday list for 2026 includes a total of 16 non-trading days, excluding weekends. With several holidays already behind us, there are still multiple breaks lined up over the coming months.

Gold slips over RS 5,000, silver plunges Rs 15,000: What's behind the sudden fall?Precious metals, often seen as safe bets in uncertain times, have unexpectedly slipped. The latest drop in gold and silver prices has left investors wondering what's driving the decline despite global tensions.
Sensex ends 185 points higher, Nifty above 22,700; HCLTech up 3%Sensex ends 185 points higher, Nifty above 22,700; HCLTech up 3%

Markets rebound: Sensex, Nifty recover losses after early crash. Here's whyEarlier in the day, both Sensex and Nifty had fallen nearly 2%, reacting to fresh comments from US President Donald Trump, who said the United States would hit Iran "extremely hard" in the coming weeks.

Form 15G, 15H gone: Will new Form 121 simplify TDS for taxpayers?Form 121 is a self-declaration that allows individuals to request that no TDS be deducted on certain types of income. This usually includes interest earned from bank deposits, post office schemes, or similar sources.

Will the new Income Tax website make filing returns simpler for you?The new website brings together all income tax-related information in one place. According to the department, it is easier to navigate, faster to use, and built to reduce confusion for taxpayers who often struggle with forms, rules and procedures.

8th Pay Commission moves ahead: Will Dehradun meeting bring your salary hike sooner?There's finally some activity around the 8th Pay Commission, and it's giving employees a reason to pay attention. With the Dehradun meeting scheduled, many are wondering, will this bring your salary revision any closer?

entertainment.headline: Dhurandhar 2 Day 16: Film surpasses Rs 1500-crore mark at the global box office Dhurandhar: The Revenge sustained strong box office momentum on its sixteenth day with a growth in earnings on Friday. The film currently trails behind Dangal, Baahubali 2: The Conclusion and Pushpa 2: The Rule in the list of all-time highest Indian global grossers.

Your Friends and Neighbors S2 first impression: Slick, sharp and slightly stretched Jon Hamm's Andrew "Coop" Cooper is once again back in the second season of Your Friends and Neighbors. The arrival of James Marsden injects fresh energy in a cocktail of existential dread and dark comedy.

Mukesh Chhabra says 3 major actors said no to Akshaye Khanna's role in Dhurandhar Casting director Mukesh Chhabra shared insights into the casting challenges for Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar (2025). He opened up on the actors who turned down Akshaye Khanna's Rehman Dakait role.

Raakaasa review: More noise than laughs in a tired templateRelease Date:Fri Apr 03, 2026 Raakaasa review: Raakaasa, directed by Manasa Sharma, blends horror and comedy but struggles with weak writing and forced humour. While performances and production design stand out, the film falls short of its promising premise.

6 years in Phulera: Makers announce start of Panchayat Season 5 shoot, share pics The makers of Panchayat marked six years of the show by announcing the start of Season 5 shoot. Jitendra Kumar, Neena Gupta and Raghubir Yadav, among others, are set to return.

I learnt to survive: Rajpal Yadav opens up on his jail days Rajpal Yadav opened up about his disciplined life in Tihar Jail. Speaking about his ongoing struggles, he thanked the film fraternity, adding that he looked forward to his upcoming film, Bhooth Bangla.

Piyush Mishra says he made obscene calls to women during struggle with alcoholism Piyush Mishra said that he made obscene calls to women during his struggle with alcoholism. He recalled how that phase affected his personal and professional life.

NCT member Mark Lee announces departure from the K-pop band, fans in shock Mark Lee has announced his departure from the popular K-pop band, NCT, ending a decade-long association with the group. Lee's decision comes into effect on April 8.

Ranbir's Ramayana looks grand, but avoid Adipurush mistakes: Vindu Dara Singh Vindu Dara Singh reacted to Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayana. While he praised the teaser as spectacular, he warned that any major deviation from the original epic.

Vikrant Massey on his gruelling TV days: Had to shoot for 110 hours non-stop Vikrant Massey opened up on the relentless schedule during his initial days as a television actor. He recalled 110 hours non-stop at that time.

Biker review: Less sport and too much drama bogs down this Sharwanand filmRelease Date:Fri Apr 03, 2026 Biker movie review: Written and directed by Abhilash Reddy, Biker starring Sharwanand, Rajashekhar and Malvika Nair has its moments of shine but lacks enough engagement to pass as compelling.

Honey Singh postpones sold-out Pune concert due to weather, new date out Honey Singh has postponed his much-awaited Pune concert due to heavy rain and thunderstorms, as per government advisory. The concert, scheduled for April 4, has now been pushed to April 18.

Amol Parashar reflects on female fans' thirsty messages and social media space Actor Amol Parashar shared his evolving view on receiving 'thirsty messages' from female fans. He emphasises creating a safe and respectful social media space where women can openly express desire without fear of judgement.

Real story behind Ravana's Pushpak Vimana: Ramayana teaser's biggest moment The Ramayana teaser introduces a grand cinematic world, but it's the brief glimpse of the Pushpak Vimana that truly stands out. Here's why this iconic flying chariot adds intrigue, depth, and a deeper mythological layer to the film's vision.

Koffee With Karan may return with Season 9: Check out what's likely to change Filmmaker Karan Johar is reportedly planning to bring back Koffee With Karan for Season 9. The upcoming season is expected to reflect changing audience preferences.

It comes from ego: Abhishek Bachchan rejects gender roles in marriage with Aishwarya Actor Abhishek Bachchan opened up about ego, masculinity, and why he rejected traditional gender roles in his marriage to Aishwarya Rai Bachchan. He also explained why he believes a partner should never have to "stop" for the other to feel successful.

Masters of the Universe trailer: Nicholas Galitzine leads live-action He-Man reboot The trailer for Masters of the Universe introduces Nicholas Galitzine as Prince Adam. Directed by Travis Knight, the film blends sci-fi spectacle with action and is set for a theatrical release in multiple languages.

Maamla Legal Hai 2 review: Ravi Kishan brings quirky chaos to the judge's chairRelease Date:Fri Apr 03, 2026 Maamla Legal Hai Season 2 series review: The second season of Maamla Legal Hai blends humour with sharp social insights, mainly through Ravi Kishan's compelling portrayal of judge Tyagi.

The Super Mario Galaxy Movie review: Bigger is not always betterRelease Date:Fri Apr 03, 2026 The Super Mario Galaxy Movie returns with vibrant visuals and energetic animation, capturing the charm of the beloved franchise with new characters and intergalactic adventures. However, the film's familiar plot and cautious storytelling make it an entertaining yet ultimately forgettable sequel.

general news.headline: We're in war: Trump says US jet downing won't derail Iran talksDonald Trump said the downing of US jets would not impact Iran talks, calling the situation "war" as military operations continue alongside diplomacy. Two US aircraft incidents in the Gulf and Iran refusing ceasefire talks in Islamabad signal rising tensions and stalled negotiations.

Second US jet A-10 crashed in Gulf after F15E crash, pilot rescuedThe incident came as a US F-15E Strike Eagle was shot down over Iran, with one crew member rescued and search efforts ongoing for the second.

Pilot, gunner of US fighter jet eject over Iran. One rescued, hunt on for the otherA pilot and a weapons systems officer of a US F-15E fighter jet that was shot down by the IRGC ejected over Iran. While one of them has been rescued, reports on the other differ. Some say that the American is in Iran's custody, with others reporting that a hunt is on.

AAP launches full-blown attack on Raghav Chadha, BJP comes out in supportSeveral Aam Aadmi Party leaders on Friday shared videos attacking Raghav Chadha for not speaking against PM Modi and the BJP-led Central government.
Donald Trump stopped 7 wars and then stopped stoppingThe man who can stop all wars cannot stop the one he started. That's Donald Trump for you.
Earthquake kills 8 in Afghanistan; tremors felt in Delhi-NCR, North IndiaStrong earthquake tremors were felt across Delhi-NCR and several parts of North India around 9.50 pm on Friday.

Live: Iran rejects US 48-hour ceasefire proposal, says semi-state mediaUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Iran has rejected a US proposal for a 48-hour ceasefire, an unnamed source told Iran's semi-official Fars News Agency. Israel pounded Beirut with airstrikes on Friday that the military said had targeted militant "infrastructure" in Lebanon's capital, as the US warned that Iran may target universities in the country as the Middle East war rages. More than 1,300 people have been killed ​in Israeli strikes and about a fifth of Lebanon's population has been displaced. Israel has issued evacuation orders covering around 15% of Lebanese territory.

Iran not willing to meet US officials in Pak, calls American demands unacceptableUS-Iran ceasefire talks have stalled after Iran refused Islamabad meeting, calling US demands unacceptable, dealing a blow to regional mediation efforts, The Wall Street Journal reported.

From Apple to auto-rickshaw: How Rakesh found freedom beyond the corporate worldA former Apple and corporate professional walked away from high-paying jobs to find peace and purpose in a very different way. His Instagram reel went viral as he explained why he chose freedom over pressure.

'Trump targetting women': Tulsi Gabbard, Karoline Leavitt to be fired next?Trump is facing backlash for firing two women officials from his male-dominated cabinet in less than a month. Reports now suggest that Tulsi Gabbard and Karoline Leavitt could be next in line of fire.

Whoever captures or kills US pilot will be commended: Iran official after jet downedIranian media announced rewards for civilians who capture the "enemy pilot alive" after Tehran claimed to have shot down an American fighter jet.

How a Nair united Muslims, Christians to dislodge India's first Communist govtIt was Kerala that saw the first elected Communist government in the world in 1957. However, an Education Bill brought about its downfall. A Nair leader converted a Church protest into a mass movement that was seized by Indira Gandhi to get the EMS Namboodiripad-led government dismissed.

6 years in Phulera: Makers announce start of Panchayat Season 5 shoot, share picsThe makers of Panchayat marked six years of the show by announcing the start of Season 5 shoot. Jitendra Kumar, Neena Gupta and Raghubir Yadav, among others, are set to return.

Minerva rout Liverpool 6-0 as Indian football finds hope in dark timesMinerva Academy's U-15 side stunned Liverpool with a dominant 6-0 win at the MIC Cup. The result offers a rare glimmer of hope for Indian football in difficult times.

PBKS hardly break sweat in 210 chase, hand CSK 5-wicket defeat at ChepaukCSK vs PBKS, IPL 2026: A 210 target proved little challenge for Punjab Kings, who cruised home despite a late wobble, as CSK's struggles at Chepauk continued with another defeat.

Northwest India to be hit by heavy rain on Saturday, mercury to dipThe IMD has forecast heavy rain and hailstorms across northwest India for April 4. Temperatures are expected to drop by five degrees Celsius in several states.

This IIT is solving the mental health crisis in students with ancient Indian knowledgeAs student suicides and stress rise across IITs, IIT Mandi is turning to Indian Knowledge Systems like yoga, meditation and classical music. Backed by research, this approach is showing how traditional practices may help improve focus, emotional balance and mental well-being on campus.

Tanker carrying Iranian crude diverts to China after signalling IndiaAn Eswatini-flagged crude oil tanker Ping Shun, with 6 lakh barrels of Iranian oil, which was India-bound, to deliver India's first shipment in seven years, has changed course to China near Gujarat's Vadinar port.

Not Iran, Trump's USA is seeing a regime changeThe US launched the war against Iran, promising a regime change in Tehran. Instead, a regime change might be unfolding in Washington DC. Trump is firing top officers in what looks like an escalating purge. Experts say more heads might roll soon.

The first 30 days of FY27: 7 smart money moves to make in AprilThe new financial year has just begun, and April is the time to take charge. The decisions you make now can shape your money journey for the rest of the year. In our new 'MONEY MAKEOVER' series, we break down simple, smart steps to get started right.

A spicy mission: Why Nasa has launched 5 hot sauces to the MoonWhen Nasa's Artemis crew launched on April 2, five bottles of hot sauce were on the menu alongside 189 other food items. The reason will surprise you.

Iran threatens UAE's Stargate AI centre as US warns of power grid strikesIran warned that it will 'completely annihilate' the Stargate AI data centre in Abu Dhabi, developed by major global companies, if the US attacks its energy plants.
Why is Pune submerged? The unusual weather pattern behind heavy rainMeteorologists say the sudden downpour is the result of a complex and unusual mix of atmospheric conditions.

Dhurandhar 2's 15-day run: Rs 1,492 crore box office; behind Pushpa 2, Baahubali 2Dhurandhar: The Revenge has finished its two-week run at the box office, creating new records and breaking the existing ones - both globally and domestically. It is just behind Pushpa 2, Baahubali 2 and Dangal on the list of top Indian films of all-time worldwide.

sports.headline: DC vs MI preview: Buoyant Delhi Capitals look to stop mighty Mumbai at home IPL 2026, DC vs MI preview: Building on the momentum of a rare winning start, the mighty Mumbai Indians arrive in Delhi with Mitchell Santner's inclusion set to bolster an already formidable unit. However, the Capitals will be no pushovers in this high-octane afternoon clash at the Arun Jaitley Stadium.

Learn to chase like Kohli: Sehwag's advice to Raghuvanshi after KKR collapse Virender Sehwag urged Angkrish Raghuvanshi to learn the art of finishing from Virat Kohli after a promising knock ended in a costly run-out during KKR's failed 267 chase.

CSK captain throws bowlers under the bus after 6th straight defeat at Chepauk Chennai Super Kings captain Ruturaj Gaikwad blamed his bowlers after the hosts failed to defend 210 runs against Punjab Kings on Friday. Gaikwad, who played a horror knock today, said that the total was enough to win the game.

PBKS hardly break sweat in 210 chase, hand CSK 5-wicket defeat at Chepauk CSK vs PBKS, IPL 2026: A 210 target proved little challenge for Punjab Kings, who cruised home despite a late wobble, as CSK's struggles at Chepauk continued with another defeat.

Minerva rout Liverpool 6-0 as Indian football finds hope in dark times Minerva Academy's U-15 side stunned Liverpool with a dominant 6-0 win at the MIC Cup. The result offers a rare glimmer of hope for Indian football in difficult times.

Ayush Mhatre fails bat test twice, umpire error sparks bizarre scenes IPL 2026, CSK vs PBKS: Chennai Super Kings batter Ayush Mhatre was at the center of a bizarre bat test controversy in the group clash before responding with a stunning half-century at Chepauk.

Gambhir has won IPL and World Cup, must be doing something right: Yuvraj India head coach Gautam Gambhir has found support from Yuvraj Singh. Speaking on SportsTak, Yuvraj said he was glad that Gambhir stuck to his methods during the T20 World Cup, despite the world telling him otherwise.

Jayawardene hits back at MI title drought talk: Some teams haven't won it forever Mahela Jayawardene, Mumbai Indians head coach, hit back at the talk around his team's recent IPL struggles, saying a five-year title drought does not take away from their success as one of the tournament's most successful franchises.

Chennai turns up for Ruturaj at toss, goes silent seeing him bat CSK vs PBKS: Chennai Super Kings fans fell silent temporarily on Friday, after their captain Ruturaj Gaikwad played a stuttering innings in their first home game. Gaikwad, got out for 28 off 22 balls.

Real Madrid get Bellingham, Militao boost before Bayern test Alvaro Arbeloa confirmed Jude Bellingham and Eder Militao have returned to match fitness ahead of Real Madrid's crucial Champions League clash with Bayern Munich, praising Militao's impact and leadership.

Italy's World Cup nightmare continues, Gattuso exits after World Cup failure Italy's World Cup woes deepened as Gennaro Gattuso stepped down after their playoff defeat, marking a third straight failure to qualify and exposing a growing crisis in Italian football.

Yuvraj Singh disappointed after SRH ignore Abhishek Sharma for leadership roleYuvraj Singh expressed disappointment over SRH's decision to appoint Ishan Kishan as stand-in captain ahead of Abhishek Sharma, saying such decisions can affect a player's mindset and sense of belonging.

Chennai turns up volume in first home game, delays CSK captain's toss interviewCSK vs PBKS: The MA Chidambaram Stadium in Chennai was at its noisiest best in Chennai Super Kings' first home game on April 3. The noise was so loud that Ruturaj Gaikwad's toss interview was stopped by broadcaster Ian Bishop.

Dad wanted me to become doctor, Irfan Pathan trusted me: Auqib NabiDC vs MI: Delhi Capitals pacer Auqib Nabi credited Irfan Pathan for his cricketing success with Jammu and Kashmir. Nabi, who starred in J&K's Ranji Trophy win last season, revealed that it was Pathan who helped him make a senior team debut.

Kohli, Shastri gave no clarity on my future, Dhoni shared the truth: YuvrajYuvraj Singh says he received no clarity on his future from team management in his final years, adding MS Dhoni's honest chat gave him perspective as he urges better communication for seniors.

CSK vs PBKS Highlights: Punjab thrash Chennai by 5 wickets at ChepaukChennai (CSK) vs Punjab (PBKS) Highlights, IPL 2026: Punjab Kings have absolutely hammered Chennai Super Kings at the MA Chidambaram Stadium. Shreyas Iyer's fifty and an aggressive start from Priyansh Arya helped the team seal a 5-wicket victory.

How did Rohit Sharma get ready for IPL 2026? MI coach breaks down the processMahela Jayawardene broke down the process adopted by Rohit Sharma as he got himself ready for the IPL 2026 campaign. Rohit scored a brilliant 78 against KKR in Mumbai's opening game.

Pant wants to make white-ball return for India: Yuvraj defends struggling batterYuvraj Singh supports Rishabh Pant's efforts to regain form and confidence to return to India's limited-overs set-up. Despite recent struggles, Pant is training closely with Yuvraj, who believes the wicketkeeper-batter is in a better mental space ahead of the IPL.

The death of Italian football: Inside Italy's fall from graceAnother World Cup missed and Italy find themselves hitting a new low following the qualifier loss to Bosnia and Herzegovina. The chain reaction that started with the Calciopoli scandal still continues to haunt Italian football, which is looking for another revival.

Rohit-Kohli deserve clarity from India management: Yuvraj Singh on 2027 WC plansYuvraj Singh has called for clarity from India's management on Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli's ODI futures. The former all-rounder stressed that clear planning is key ahead of the 2027 World Cup.

Match Preview: Jittery Chennai hope to conjure magic at home-turf vs PunjabIPL 2026: Chennai Super Kings are set to play their first home game this season. The Ruturaj Gaikwad-led side will take on a strong Punjab Kings outfit, hoping that fortress Chepauk provides them with their first win of the season.

Dhoni returns to CSK training: Will he play Chepauk clash vs PBKS?IPL 2026: MS Dhoni's return to CSK training has raised hopes of an early comeback after injury. However, signs from practice suggest the veteran may take a cautious route before match return.

As fuel rates soar, Shahid Afridi urges Pakistanis to back govt, appeals for calmShahid Afridi supports Pakistan's fuel price hike amid the oil crisis in the country. The Pakistan government decided to hike the price of diesel and petrol in the country, citing the surge in international crude oil prices.

CSK will back youngsters: Ruturaj Gaikwad wants less pressure on new facesIPL 2026: Ruturaj Gaikwad has urged patience as CSK embrace a new, youthful core in IPL 2026. After a heavy loss to RR, the focus shifts from results to growth and freedom for young players.

You'll come from Virar? CSK's Ayush Mhatre relives emotional Rohit Sharma meetIndia U19 captain Ayush Mhatre recalled his dream meeting with Rohit Sharma at his residence and explained how the former Indian skipper spent two hours just chatting to him. Mhatre met with Rohit ahead of the England tour last year.

Wake up KKR! Make those tough calls early before the season slips awayIPL 2026: KKR have already got their backs to the wall after just two games, with the combination still being a major issue. Can they find some inspiration from their 2012 season and get back on track with some big calls?

I was just talking with President, Tiger Woods is heard telling cops in videoTiger Woods' DUI arrest has taken a fresh turn after bodycam footage revealed he claimed to have spoken with Donald Trump moments after the crash. The golf legend has since stepped away from the sport to focus on his health.

Abhishek Sharma handed hefty fine by IPL for breach of conduct in KKR vs SRHAbhishek Sharma has been fined 25 percent of his match fees and handed one demerit point after SRH's win over KKR at the Eden Gardens on Thursday, April 2. Abhishek was sanctioned for breaching IPL's Code of Conduct during the game.

SRH bowling not weak as you think: Nitish Reddy shuts critics after KKR winIPL 2026: Nitish Kumar Reddy shut down criticism of SRH's bowling unit after a dominant win over KKR. The all-rounder insisted their attack is as strong as their much-hyped batting lineup.

IPL 2026 Play of the Day: How Angkrish's calls hurt KKRIPL 2026, KKR vs SRH: Kolkata Knight Riders' chase unraveled after a pair of costly misjudgments, as lapses in running between the wickets brought about their undoing with Cameron Green and Angkrish Raghuvanshi falling to avoidable run-outs that stalled the innings and handed the game firmly to Sunrisers Hyderabad.

Nitish's redemption night at Eden: From injury blues to SRH hero in KolkataNitish Kumar Reddy overcame self-doubt and injury woes to emerge as SRH'S hero at the Eden Gardens as they beat KKR. Nitish starred with both bat and ball to change the course of the game and get the win.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
